comment.body: The no intrinsic value argument can, with almost equal weight be applied to growth stocks. Difference is we all accept their utility: is exposure to future value and cash flow. That is their value. So if bitcoin has any utility distasteful or not it has value. If we don’t include it’s utility as alternative store of value then the vast majority of gold’s market cap has no value because so little of it is derived from its non monetary use. BTC is a finance-ilisation of culture
comment.raw_body: The no intrinsic value argument can, with almost equal weight be applied to growth stocks. Difference is we all accept their utility: is exposure to future value and cash flow. comment.body: Totally agree. But beauty and elegance in itself don't have a value. And if you put it differently: in most cases you don't need a distributed ledger (aka a database). A well set up central database with redundancies will be more energy efficient and easier to handle. You really only need this if you cannot trust the other parties. Which brings you back to criminals, libertarians etc. But for a regular industry usage a blockchain is just a very inefficient tool to handle data storage. Same with the related smart contracts. No reason why this cannot be solved without blockchain

comment.body:
It's actually the other way around:
So many SPX names have calls sold against them, but TSLA has calls *bought*. The index wants to move XYZ amount, and the constituent parts must balance it out. Note that the news today was that Tesla sales in Europe are down 25% YoY. BYD in China is kicking its ass at lower prices with FSD included. The company makes cars and, if valued like a no-growth car co would be $30/share. But the QQQ flows gotta find a home.

comment.body: Sure - but i think this is one of those truisms that can be deceptive if you want to trade based off it. It should close, but when? After we're 500 points higher? In 6 months?
